title: Alnylam — ATTR — briefing Q4-2025
html:
ATTR Amyloidosis Briefing: Q4 2025

Quarter Headline

AMVUTTRA's ATTR-CM franchise surpasses $2.3B for full year 2025, Alnylam turns profitable, and the company launches "Alnylam 2030" strategy targeting $10B+ in revenue. ATTRUBY closes its first full year at $362M. The four-way ATTR-CM competitive landscape is fully established.

Key Developments

1. Alnylam Full Year 2025 Results (Reported Feb 2026)

  • AMVUTTRA FY 2025 revenue: $2,314M (103% growth YoY)
  • Q4 2025 AMVUTTRA revenue: $827M (151% growth vs. Q4 2024)
  • ONPATTRO FY 2025: $173M (declining as expected, transitioning to AMVUTTRA)
  • TTR franchise total: $2,487M
  • Alnylam achieved profitability for the first time
  • 2026 TTR guidance: $4,400-4,700M (83% growth at midpoint)

What Alnylam management said on the Q4 2025 / FY 2025 call:

  1. "We are the RNAi leader in cardiometabolic disease" — management elevated AMVUTTRA beyond rare disease into the cardiometabolic category, signaling ambitions to compete with mainstream cardiology therapies.
  2. 2026 TTR guidance set at $4,400-4,700M, but flagged Q1 2026 would be "more modest" QoQ growth due to fewer shipping weeks, insurance resets, and Germany price cuts (~$25M/quarter). This deliberate expectations management suggests confidence in full-year trajectory.
  3. HELIOS-B positioning: "The most comprehensive evidence in ATTR-CM" — framing the data package as a moat against both stabilizers and future silencer entrants.
  4. On Attruby cannibalization: "Stabilizers and silencers serve different clinical needs; many patients will be on both" — introducing the combination therapy narrative, which reframes Attruby from competitor to co-therapy.

Key analyst Q&A signals:

  1. Analysts pushed on 2026 guidance achievability — Alnylam responded: "We have line of sight to the numbers." The specificity of the $4,400-4,700M range and lack of hedging suggests internal forecasts exceed the guidance midpoint.
  2. CARDIO-TTRansform question drew a notable response: "We're prepared for any outcome" — implying Alnylam has pre-planned messaging strategies for both positive and negative Wainua data.
  3. Analysts asked "Is the $10B target realistic without another major approval?" — Alnylam: "Our pipeline has multiple shots on goal." The 2030 strategy ($10B+) depends on zilebesiran (hypertension) and mivelsiran (Alzheimer's), not just TTR growth.

2. Alnylam 2030 Strategy Launch (January 2026)

  • Announced at JPM Healthcare Conference, January 11, 2026
  • Target: next era of growth and patient impact through TTR leadership and pipeline scale
  • TTR franchise positioned as engine to fund pipeline diversification
  • Signals long-term commitment to ATTR -- research engagement will be sustained and growing

3. ATTRUBY Full Year 2025 Performance

  • BridgeBio reported $362.4M full year 2025 ATTRUBY revenue
  • 6,629 unique patient prescriptions from 1,632 prescribers by Dec 31, 2025
  • BridgeBio projects continued growth; 7,804 scripts from 1,856 prescribers by Feb 2026
  • Claims "first-choice therapy for newly diagnosed ATTR-CM"
  • International launches via Bayer (Europe as BEYONTTRA) and Alexion (Japan) are underway

4. Q4 2025 Headwinds and Concerns

  • US AMVUTTRA message effectiveness declined sharply across nearly all messages (10-31 pts) in Q4 2024 PET data, suggesting message fatigue
  • Alnylam expects mid-single-digit net price decrease for AMVUTTRA in US for 2026
  • Germany price cuts (~$25M quarterly reduction flagged)
  • Q1 2026 expected to show "more modest" sequential TTR growth due to fewer shipping weeks and insurance resets

5. Competitive Dynamics Snapshot (End 2025)

Metric AMVUTTRA ATTRUBY VYNDAMAX Wainua
2025 Revenue $2,314M $362M ~$5.4B (tafamidis total, declining) Pre-CM approval
Patients >12,000 ~7,000 Incumbent base hATTR-PN only
Key advantage Only silencer for CM+PN; mortality data "Best stabilizer"; oral; fast onset Incumbent; oral; longest track record Self-administered; pending CM data
Key vulnerability SC injection; price pressure Limited to CM; no PN data Patent cliff 2028; declining share Pending CARDIO-TTRansform results

6. Competitor Earnings Intelligence — Q4 2025

Revenue Snapshot — Q4 2025

Company Brand Q4 2025 Revenue FY 2025 Revenue Key Signal
Pfizer Vyndaqel/Vyndamax ~$1.3B ~$5.4B +18% YoY; acknowledges AMVUTTRA as competitive but positions tafamidis as "the oral option with deepest real-world evidence"
BridgeBio Attruby (acoramidis) ~$80M $362M Accelerating uptake; international launches via Bayer (EU) and Alexion (Japan)
Ionis/AstraZeneca Wainua (eplontersen) ~$30M ~$80M (est.) Slow ramp in hATTR-PN; CARDIO-TTRansform primary completion extended to April 2026
AMVUTTRA $827M $2,314M +151% YoY Q4; TTR franchise crossed $2.5B for the year

Pfizer — Vyndaqel/Vyndamax (~$1.3B quarterly, ~$5.4B FY2025)

  1. Tafamidis franchise peaks but competitive pressure acknowledged: ~$1.3B in Q4 2025 represents the highest quarterly revenue for tafamidis, but Pfizer explicitly acknowledged AMVUTTRA's ATTR-CM approval (Jan 2025) as a competitive factor. Management positioned tafamidis as "the oral option with the deepest real-world evidence" — a deliberate framing that concedes the efficacy argument to silencers while defending on convenience and track record.

  2. Patent cliff 2028 looming: Pfizer's tafamidis patent expires in 2028, creating a structural headwind. Even without competition from AMVUTTRA and Attruby, tafamidis would face biosimilar/generic erosion within 3 years. This accelerates the urgency for cardiologists to evaluate next-generation therapies.

  3. Growth moderating: +18% YoY is solid but decelerating from +20% in Q3. The combination of AMVUTTRA capturing new starts and Attruby converting tafamidis switchers is beginning to show in growth rates, even though the absolute revenue remains enormous.

Implication for Alnylam: Pfizer's defensive positioning ("oral with deepest evidence") reveals their competitive strategy: they will not fight AMVUTTRA on efficacy/mechanism but on convenience and physician comfort. Alnylam's counter should emphasize that ATTR-CM is a progressive, fatal disease where mechanism matters more than convenience — patients deserve the therapy that halts TTR production, not just stabilizes it.

BridgeBio — Attruby (~$80M quarterly, $362M FY2025)

  1. Accelerating uptake in Q4: $80M quarterly revenue represents a significant step-up from ~$50M in Q3. BridgeBio's first full year (FY2025: $362M) exceeded most analyst expectations and established Attruby as a viable commercial product.

  2. "1-month onset" messaging is resonating: BridgeBio is targeting tafamidis switchers with "1-month onset" messaging (vs tafamidis 6+ months to biomarker improvement). This is a compelling within-class argument that is fragmenting the stabilizer market.

  3. International expansion underway: Bayer launching as BEYONTTRA in Europe; Alexion launching in Japan. BridgeBio is building a global stabilizer franchise that will compete with both tafamidis (incumbent) and AMVUTTRA (challenger) internationally.

  4. 7,000+ patients by year-end: 6,629 unique prescriptions from 1,632 prescribers signals deep physician adoption. Attruby is not a niche product — it's becoming a mainstream ATTR-CM therapy.

Implication for Alnylam: Attruby's $362M first year is a warning signal. The "better stabilizer" positioning is succeeding — some patients who might have been candidates for AMVUTTRA are instead choosing Attruby because it's oral and claims faster onset. AMVUTTRA must differentiate on mechanism (silencer vs stabilizer) and outcomes (mortality reduction from HELIOS-B), not just efficacy.

Ionis/AstraZeneca — Wainua (~$30M quarterly)

  1. Slow ramp continues in hATTR-PN: ~$30M quarterly represents modest growth from $20M in Q3. Wainua is competing with AMVUTTRA in the polyneuropathy space but gaining limited traction against the established silencer.

  2. CARDIO-TTRansform delays create uncertainty: Primary completion extended to April 2026 pushes the potential ATTR-CM data readout into mid-2026. Each quarter of delay benefits AMVUTTRA by allowing more cardiologists to establish AMVUTTRA as their default silencer choice.

  3. The binary catalyst remains: If CARDIO-TTRansform is positive, Wainua becomes the second silencer option in ATTR-CM, potentially valued for self-administration convenience. If negative, AMVUTTRA's silencer monopoly in CM is cemented for the foreseeable future.

Implication for Alnylam: The CARDIO-TTRansform delay is tactically favorable for AMVUTTRA. Every quarter that AMVUTTRA is the sole silencer in ATTR-CM builds prescriber habit and treatment algorithm stickiness. Alnylam should use this window to lock in cardiologist mindshare.

Synthesis: The Four-Way ATTR-CM Landscape at Year-End 2025

The $8B+ ATTR-CM market (tafamidis $5.4B + AMVUTTRA $2.3B + Attruby $362M + Wainua ~$80M in PN) is the richest competitive landscape in rare disease. Three narratives are competing:

  1. Stabilizer incumbency (Pfizer): "Oral, proven, 10+ years of data." Defensive but backed by $5.4B in annual revenue.
  2. Next-gen stabilizer (BridgeBio): "Better stabilizer — faster onset, superior biomarkers, still oral." Fragmenting the stabilizer market from within.
  3. Gene silencing (Alnylam): "Mechanism superiority — halt TTR production, not just stabilize. Mortality benefit." The scientific high ground, but requires quarterly SC injection.

The question for 2026: Does the market converge on silencers (AMVUTTRA wins long-term) or does the stabilizer market segment into "good enough" and "better" stabilizers (Attruby captures patients who might otherwise move to AMVUTTRA)? CARDIO-TTRansform adds a fourth dimension — if positive, Wainua fragments the silencer segment the same way Attruby is fragmenting the stabilizer segment.

ZoomRx Research Status

  • US PET Q4 2024 data shows concerning message effectiveness declines -- potential strategic discussion point
  • ATU Q4 2025 wave completed (90 cardiologists, ATTR-CM)
  • PCA H2 2025 wave delivered
  • Target list segmentation change initiated (Ultra/High/Medium/Low/Exploratory tiers)
  • WAINUA fielding paused in PET after hitting quota; to resume pending CARDIO-TTRansform

Signals for Vinoth

  • Alnylam's profitability and 2030 strategy signal sustained, growing research budget. Position for multi-year engagement expansion
  • Message fatigue in US PET is a strategic insight that should be proactively raised with Alnylam -- opportunity for message optimization consulting
  • ATTRUBY's $362M first year validates the competitive tracking investment. BridgeBio is a viable standalone client opportunity
  • Target list segmentation change will affect trend comparability -- establish methodological bridge plan with the team
